quiz.title: Grade-10 Physics: Midterm Review Quiz 9
quiz.subtitle: Comprehensive Review — Kinematics through Simple Machines
quiz.subject: Physics

questions.question: A particle traveling along a straight engineering test track passes a reference marker with an initial velocity of $18.5\text{ m/s}$ and begins to decelerate uniformly. If the particle comes to a complete rest exactly $4.20\text{ s}$ after passing the marker, calculate the total linear distance it traveled during this braking period.

A structural engineer evaluates a reinforced concrete foundation block designed to support heavy machinery. The mass of the block is recorded on the blueprint as $2.35\text{ tons}$. Convert this mass value directly into kilograms ($\text{kg}$), given that $1\text{ ton} = 1,000\text{ kg}$.

A child pulls a toy wagon across a smooth horizontal sidewalk at a constant speed using a handle tilted upward at $35.0^{\circ}$ relative to the ground. Analyze the nature of the work done on the wagon by the horizontal component of this pull force over a displacement.
questions.explanation: The horizontal component of the pull acts in the same direction as the displacement. Work is positive.

questions.question: A high-altitude scientific research balloon carries an instrumentation payload with a mass of $84.0\text{ kg}$ to a stable altitude of $4,200\text{ m}$ above sea level. Determine the gravitational potential energy stored in the payload relative to sea level. ($g = 9.80\text{ m/s}^2$)

A $1,400\text{ kg}$ car is coasting along a level test track at $26.0\text{ m/s}$. The driver applies the brakes, and a constant horizontal braking force of $6,800\text{ N}$ acts on the vehicle until its speed is reduced to $12.0\text{ m/s}$. Use the work-energy relationship to calculate the distance the car travels while slowing down.

A $0.145\text{ kg}$ hockey puck is gliding across frictionless ice at $14.0\text{ m/s}$ due north. A player strikes the puck, delivering a brief impact that changes its velocity to $28.0\text{ m/s}$ due south. Calculate the total impulse imparted to the puck.
questions.explanation: North (+). $\Delta v = -28.0 - 14.0 = -42.0\text{ m/s}$. $\Delta p = 0.145 \times (-42.0) = -6.09\text{ N s}$ (south).

questions.question: An industrial overhead crane lifts a heavy $1,150\text{ kg}$ steel beam vertically upward through $16.0\text{ m}$ at constant speed. The electric winch requires exactly $24.0\text{ s}$ to complete this lift. Calculate the average useful power output generated by the motor. ($g = 9.80\text{ m/s}^2$)

A hydraulic press mechanism performs $4.85 \times 10^4\text{ J}$ of useful mechanical work during a manufacturing stamping cycle. The system draws $7.50 \times 10^4\text{ J}$ of input energy from its power supply. Calculate the percentage efficiency of the hydraulic system.

A $0.0250\text{ kg}$ metal slug is fired horizontally into a stationary $3.80\text{ kg}$ block of soft wood resting on a low-friction table. The slug embeds itself in the wood, and the combined system slides forward at $2.40\text{ m/s}$. Calculate the initial speed of the slug before entering the block.

A mechanic uses a wheel and axle system to hoist structural components. The radius of the outer wheel is $45.0\text{ cm}$ and the radius of the inner axle is $7.50\text{ cm}$. Calculate the ideal mechanical advantage (IMA) of this machine assembly.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You're well-prepared across all units!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review the topics where you made errors.
scoreMessages.50: Keep studying! Focus on impulse with direction changes and work-energy with partial braking.
scoreMessages.0: Significant review needed. Revisit kinematics, energy, momentum, and simple machines.
